response.justification: Bogdan brings 20+ years of strong hands-on technical experience, genuine developer tooling contributions (test runners, CI/CD pipelines, MCP frameworks), and community knowledge-sharing through workshops and talks. However, the role is fundamentally a people management position — setting performance expectations, conducting 1:1s, developing engineers — and the resume contains zero evidence of managing direct reports at any point in his career. Technical depth alone does not bend this into a management-ready profile; the absence of people leadership is a disqualifying gap for a Level II Manager role.
strengths: 20+ years of diverse software development experience across web, mobile, embedded, and infrastructure domains far exceeds the 8-year minimum

Concrete developer tooling contributions: Trial (test runner), Fluent-Asserts (TDD library), CI/CD infrastructure at Altom, and MCP/agentic tooling at re:cinq align directly with the role's focus on engineering productivity
Knowledge-sharing through Selenium courses, workshops, and conference talks demonstrates the coaching instinct that underpins effective engineering management
concerns: No evidence of people management — no mention of managing direct reports, conducting performance reviews, or owning headcount; this is the core function of the role
Technical leadership has been informal and individual-contributor-scoped (Staff IC, Founder of a solo OSS project) rather than organizational or team-level
No demonstrated experience with roadmap ownership, cross-team alignment, or driving organizational change at the team or department level
